SELECT 
    account_id, 
    MAX(amount) AS max_transaction, 
    MIN(amount) AS min_transaction,
    MAX(amount) - MIN(amount) AS amount_difference
FROM 
    trans
GROUP BY 
    account_id 
HAVING 
    COUNT(*) >= 2 
    AND (MAX(amount) - MIN(amount)) > 12000
ORDER BY 
    amount_difference DESC;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_loan_summary()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Update the loan summary table with the correct count of active loans
    INSERT INTO loan_summary (account_id, total_loan_count, last_status_change)
    VALUES (NEW.account_id, 
           (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM loan WHERE account_id = NEW.account_id AND status = 'A'),
           CURRENT_DATE)
    ON CONFLICT (account_id) 
    DO UPDATE SET 
        total_loan_count = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM loan WHERE account_id = NEW.account_id AND status = 'A'),
        last_status_change = CURRENT_DATE,
        last_updated = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP;
    
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER tr_update_loan_summary
AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON loan
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION update_loan_summary();

-- Option 1: Create a new unique index that matches your ON CONFLICT clause
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX unique_event ON event(event_name, location, event_date) 
WHERE event_name IS NOT NULL AND location IS NOT NULL;

-- Then your UPSERT would work:
INSERT INTO event (event_id, event_name, location, event_date) 
VALUES ('test1', 'test_name', 'test_location', 'test_date')
ON CONFLICT (event_name, location, event_date) 
DO UPDATE SET 
  event_id = 'test1', 
  event_name = 'test_name', 
  location = 'test_location', 
  event_date = 'test_date';

-- OR Option 2: Modify your UPSERT to use one of your existing constraints
INSERT INTO event (event_id, event_name, location, event_date) 
VALUES ('test1', 'test_name', 'test_location', 'test_date')
ON CONFLICT (event_name, event_date) 
DO UPDATE SET 
  event_id = 'test1', 
  event_name = 'test_name', 
  location = 'test_location', 
  event_date = 'test_date';

WITH updated_transactions AS (
    UPDATE transactions_1k 
    SET Amount = 100 
    FROM (
        SELECT TransactionID 
        FROM transactions_1k 
        WHERE Amount = 50 
        ORDER BY Date 
        LIMIT 100 
        FOR UPDATE
    ) sub 
    WHERE transactions_1k.TransactionID = sub.TransactionID 
    RETURNING transactions_1k.*
)
SELECT ut.transactionid, ut.amount, ut.date, c.segment, c.currency
FROM updated_transactions ut
JOIN customers c ON ut.customerid = c.customerid;

SELECT u.id, 
       u.displayname,
       COUNT(DISTINCT pl.relatedpostid) as premium_referrals
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN posts p ON u.id = p.owneruserid
LEFT JOIN postlinks pl ON p.id = pl.postid
LEFT JOIN posts referred_post ON pl.relatedpostid = referred_post.id
LEFT JOIN users referred_user ON referred_post.owneruserid = referred_user.id
LEFT JOIN badges b ON referred_user.id = b.userid AND b.name = 'Premium'
WHERE b.id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY u.id, u.displayname
ORDER BY premium_referrals DESC;

WITH user_refs AS (
    -- Combine all references for each user
    SELECT id, username, ref1, ref2
    FROM users
),
asset_owners AS (
    -- Get all assets with their owners
    SELECT a.id, a.name, a.owner
    FROM assets a
),
user_groups AS (
    -- Create connected groups of users based on shared references
    SELECT 
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ur.id) AS ids,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ur.username) AS usernames,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ur.ref1) AS refs1,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ur.ref2) AS refs2,
        COALESCE(ao.owner, ur.ref1, ur.ref2) AS owner_ref
    FROM user_refs ur
    LEFT JOIN asset_owners ao ON ao.owner = ur.ref1 OR ao.owner = ur.ref2
    GROUP BY COALESCE(ao.owner, ur.ref1, ur.ref2)
)
SELECT 
    ug.ids,
    ug.usernames,
    ug.refs1,
    ug.refs2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_count,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_ids
FROM user_groups ug
LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.owner = ug.owner_ref
GROUP BY ug.ids, ug.usernames, ug.refs1, ug.refs2, ug.owner_ref
ORDER BY MIN(COALESCE(a.id, 0));

WITH attendance_counts AS (
    SELECT link_to_event, COUNT(link_to_member) AS attendance_count 
    FROM attendance 
    GROUP BY link_to_event
)
SELECT 
    e.event_id,
    e.event_name,
    ac.attendance_count AS attendance_table_count,
    b.amount AS budget_attendee_count
FROM event e
JOIN attendance_counts ac ON e.event_id = ac.link_to_event
JOIN budget b ON e.event_id = b.link_to_event
WHERE ac.attendance_count != b.amount
AND b.category = 'Attendees';

    CASE 
        WHEN date < 500101 THEN '20' || TO_CHAR(date, 'FM000000') 
        ELSE '19' || TO_CHAR(date, 'FM000000')

WITH priority_accounts AS (
  SELECT
    account.account_id,
    account.frequency,
    l.loan_id,
    l.date AS loan_date,
    0 AS priority
  FROM account
  LEFT JOIN loan l
    ON account.account_id = l.account_id
  WHERE account.district_id = '18'
    AND l.date >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '48 hours')
  ORDER BY l.date DESC NULLS LAST
  LIMIT 6
)
SELECT * FROM priority_accounts
UNION ALL
SELECT
  account.account_id,
  account.frequency,
  l.loan_id,
  l.date AS loan_date,
  1 AS priority
FROM account
LEFT JOIN loan l
  ON account.account_id = l.account_id
WHERE account.district_id = '18'
  AND account.account_id NOT IN (SELECT account_id FROM priority_accounts)
ORDER BY priority, loan_date DESC NULLS LAST;

    date_trunc('year', MAX(date))::date AS max_year

)
SELECT 
  to_char(generate_series, 'YYYY') AS year
FROM 
  date_range,
  generate_series(
    min_year, 
    max_year, 
    interval '1 year'
  );

WITH distinct_dates AS (
    -- Get distinct release dates
    SELECT DISTINCT releasedate 
    FROM sets_releaseinfo
),
date_groups AS (
    -- Identify groups of consecutive dates
    SELECT 
        releasedate,
        releasedate - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY releasedate))::integer AS date_group
    FROM distinct_dates
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS consecutive_days,
    MIN(releasedate) AS start_date,
    MAX(releasedate) AS end_date
FROM date_groups
GROUP BY date_group
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY consecutive_days DESC, start_date;

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- First, identify cards that have no previous card (starting points)
starting_points AS (
    SELECT id
    FROM collection
    WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT nextCardId FROM collection WHERE nextCardId IS NOT NULL)
),
-- Then build paths starting from these cards
path_cte AS (
    -- Base case: start with cards that have no previous card
    SELECT id, nextCardId, id::TEXT AS path
    FROM collection
    WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM starting_points)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: follow the nextCardId links
    SELECT p.id, c.nextCardId, p.path || ' --> ' || c.nextCardId::TEXT
    FROM path_cte p
    JOIN collection c ON p.nextCardId = c.id
    WHERE c.nextCardId IS NOT NULL
)
-- Select the complete paths (those that end with a card having no next card)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN c.nextCardId IS NULL THEN path
        ELSE path || ' --> ' || c.nextCardId::TEXT
    END AS complete_path
FROM path_cte p
JOIN collection c ON p.nextCardId = c.id
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM path_cte p2 
    WHERE p2.id = p.id AND LENGTH(p2.path) > LENGTH(p.path)
)
ORDER BY SPLIT_PART(path, ' --> ', 1)::INTEGER;

SELECT 
    tc.table_schema || '.' || tc.table_name AS referencing_table,
    kcu.column_name AS referencing_column,
    ccu.table_schema || '.' || ccu.table_name AS referenced_table,
    ccu.column_name AS referenced_column,
    tc.constraint_name,
    pg_get_constraintdef(pgc.oid) AS constraint_definition
FROM 
    information_schema.table_constraints tc
JOIN 
    information_schema.key_column_usage kcu
    ON tc.constraint_name = kcu.constraint_name
    AND tc.table_schema = kcu.table_schema
JOIN 
    information_schema.constraint_column_usage ccu
    ON ccu.constraint_name = tc.constraint_name
    AND ccu.table_schema = tc.table_schema
JOIN 
    pg_constraint pgc
    ON pgc.conname = tc.constraint_name
WHERE 
    tc.constraint_type = 'FOREIGN KEY' 
    AND ccu.table_name = 'users'
ORDER BY 
    tc.table_name, 
    kcu.column_name;

SELECT 
    t.trans_id,
    t.account_id,
    t.date,
    t.bank,
    (SELECT nt.bank 
     FROM trans nt 
     WHERE nt.account_id = t.account_id 
       AND nt.date >= t.date 
       AND nt.bank IS NOT NULL
     ORDER BY nt.date ASC
     LIMIT 1) AS next_bank
FROM trans t
ORDER BY t.account_id, t.date;

SELECT 
    t.*,
    (SELECT nt.bank 
     FROM trans nt 
     WHERE nt.account_id = t.account_id 
       AND nt.date >= t.date 
       AND nt.bank IS NOT NULL
     ORDER BY nt.date ASC
     LIMIT 1) AS next_bank
FROM trans t
ORDER BY t.account_id, t.date;

SELECT t.team_api_id, t.team_short_name, ta.buildupplayspeed
FROM team t
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT team_api_id, buildupplayspeed
    FROM team_attributes
    WHERE (team_api_id, date) IN (
        SELECT team_api_id, MAX(date) as latest_date
        FROM team_attributes
        GROUP BY team_api_id
    )
) ta ON t.team_api_id = ta.team_api_id;

WITH combined_data AS (
    SELECT date_trunc('year', date) as action_year, 'transaction' as type
    FROM trans
    UNION ALL
    SELECT date_trunc('year', date) as action_year, 'loan' as type
    FROM loan
)
SELECT 
    action_year as year, 
    COUNT(*) as count
FROM combined_data
GROUP BY action_year
ORDER BY action_year;

WITH expanded_transactions AS (
    SELECT 
        t.CustomerID, 
        t.Amount,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            ORDER BY t.CustomerID, t.Amount, t.TransactionID
        ) AS overall_row_num
    FROM transactions_1k t
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL generate_series(1, CAST(t.Amount AS INTEGER)) AS gs(n)
)
SELECT 
    CustomerID, 
    Amount,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY overall_row_num) AS rn
FROM expanded_transactions
WHERE overall_row_num <= 5
ORDER BY overall_row_num;

SELECT 
    t.k_symbol, 
    t.operation, 
    t.amount, 
    t.balance, 
    a.frequency 
FROM trans t
INNER JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT 
        k_symbol, 
        MIN(date) as min_date
    FROM trans
    WHERE account_id = 1 AND type = 'PRIJEM'
    GROUP BY k_symbol
) min_dates ON t.k_symbol = min_dates.k_symbol AND t.date = min_dates.min_date
WHERE t.account_id = 1 AND t.type = 'PRIJEM'

WITH purchase_months AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS month_date
    FROM purchase
    GROUP BY user_id, purchase_date
),
streaks AS (
    SELECT
        user_id,
        month_date,
        month_date - (INTERVAL '1 month' * ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_date)) AS streak_group
    FROM purchase_months
),
streak_lengths AS (
    SELECT
        user_id,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streaks
    GROUP BY user_id, streak_group
),
max_streaks AS (
    SELECT
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS max_streak_length
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
SELECT
    max_streak_length AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM max_streaks
GROUP BY max_streak_length
ORDER BY max_streak_length DESC;

WITH tab_with_cardid AS (
   SELECT 
       cardid,
       -- Extract pure_cardid by removing the substring between first and second hyphens
       CONCAT(
           SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 1),
           '-',
           SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 3),
           CASE 
               WHEN SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 4) != '' THEN CONCAT('-', SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 4)) 
               ELSE '' 
           END,
           CASE 
               WHEN SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 5) != '' THEN CONCAT('-', SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 5)) 
               ELSE '' 
           END
       ) AS pure_cardid,
       type,
       last_refresh_date
    FROM card_info
), ranked_records AS (
    SELECT 
        cardid, 
        pure_cardid,
        type,
        last_refresh_date,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY pure_cardid ORDER BY type) AS rn
    FROM tab_with_cardid
)
SELECT 
    cardid, 
    pure_cardid,
    type,
    last_refresh_date
FROM ranked_records
WHERE rn = 1;

WITH LatestRatings AS (
    SELECT 
        player_api_id,
        date,
        overall_rating,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY player_api_id ORDER BY date DESC) as rn
    FROM Player_Attributes
)
SELECT 
    player_api_id,
    date,
    overall_rating
FROM LatestRatings
WHERE rn = 1;

SELECT pa.player_api_id, pa.date, pa.overall_rating
FROM Player_Attributes pa
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT player_api_id, MAX(date) as max_date
    FROM Player_Attributes
    GROUP BY player_api_id
) latest ON pa.player_api_id = latest.player_api_id AND pa.date = latest.max_date;

    id,

    CASE 
        WHEN age ~ '^[0-9]+$' THEN age::numeric 
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS numeric_age
FROM users
WHERE age IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, displayname, age FROM users;

BEGIN
    INSERT INTO profiles (id, creationdate, websiteurl) 
    SELECT 
        NEW.id::varchar, 
        NEW.creationdate::text, 
        CASE 
            WHEN NEW.websiteurl IS NULL THEN NULL
            ELSE substring(NEW.websiteurl from 'http://([^.]+)')
        END;
    RETURN NEW;
END;

SELECT *,
    (CASE WHEN availability = 'paper' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
     CASE WHEN bordercolor = 'black' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
     CASE WHEN rarity = 'uncommon' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
     CASE WHEN type LIKE '%Creature%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS matching_criteria_count
FROM cards
WHERE (CASE WHEN availability = 'paper' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
       CASE WHEN bordercolor = 'black' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
       CASE WHEN rarity = 'uncommon' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
       CASE WHEN type LIKE '%Creature%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 3
ORDER BY matching_criteria_count DESC;

ALTER TABLE hero_power ADD CONSTRAINT unique_active_hero_power 
UNIQUE (hero_id, power_id, (CASE WHEN active = true THEN 1 ELSE random() END));

WITH basic_ny_users AS (
    SELECT user_id, email, SUM(revenue) as total_revenue
    FROM orders
    WHERE segment = 'basic' AND destination = 'New York'
    GROUP BY user_id, email
    HAVING SUM(revenue) > 2000
),
miami_users AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT user_id
    FROM orders
    WHERE destination = 'Miami'
)
SELECT DISTINCT user_id, email
FROM orders
WHERE 
    -- Condition 1: Luxury segment in New York
    (segment = 'luxury' AND destination = 'New York')
    OR 
    -- Condition 2: Luxury segment in London
    (segment = 'luxury' AND destination = 'London')
UNION
-- Condition 3: Basic segment in New York with revenue > $2000
SELECT user_id, email
FROM basic_ny_users
UNION
-- Condition 4: Users who never purchased in Miami
SELECT DISTINCT user_id, email
FROM orders
WHERE user_id NOT IN (SELECT user_id FROM miami_users)

SELECT version 
FROM cars 
ORDER BY 
    -- Major version
    CAST(SPLIT_PART(version, '.', 1) AS INTEGER) DESC,
    -- Minor version
    CAST(SPLIT_PART(version, '.', 2) AS INTEGER) DESC,
    -- Patch version
    CAST(SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(version, '.', 3), '-', 1) AS INTEGER) DESC,
    -- Build number after the first hyphen
    CASE 
        WHEN POSITION('-' IN version) > 0 
        THEN CAST(NULLIF(SPLIT_PART(SUBSTRING(version FROM POSITION('-' IN version) + 1), '-', 1), '') AS INTEGER) 
        ELSE 0 
    END DESC,
    -- Any remaining text (like 'test' or 'dev') - sort alphabetically
    SUBSTRING(version FROM POSITION('-' IN version)) ASC
LIMIT 1;

SELECT d.driverid, 
       COALESCE(SUM(r.points), 0) AS total_points
FROM drivers d
LEFT JOIN results r ON d.driverid = r.driverid
GROUP BY d.driverid
ORDER BY total_points DESC

WITH alignment_counts AS (
    SELECT A.alignment, COUNT(S.id) as total_count
    FROM superhero S
    JOIN alignment A ON S.alignment_id = A.id
    GROUP BY A.alignment
)
SELECT 
    A.alignment,
    AC.total_count as total_by_alignment,
    R.race,
    COUNT(S.id) as count_by_alignment_race
FROM superhero S
JOIN alignment A ON S.alignment_id = A.id
JOIN race R ON S.race_id = R.id
JOIN alignment_counts AC ON A.alignment = AC.alignment
GROUP BY A.alignment, AC.total_count, R.race
ORDER BY A.alignment, R.race;

SELECT 
    driverId, 
    points, 
    lead(driverId) OVER (ORDER BY points ASC) AS "lead(driverId) with order", 
    array_agg(driverId) OVER (ORDER BY points ASC) AS "array_agg(driverId) with order", 
    lead(driverId) OVER (ORDER BY driverId) AS "lead(driverId) without order", 
    array_agg(driverId) OVER () AS "array_agg(driverId) without order" 
FROM 
    results 
WHERE 
    raceId = 19 
ORDER BY 
    driverId ASC

SELECT 
  raceid,
  COUNT(*) AS total_pitstops,
  SUM(CAST(SUBSTRING(duration FROM '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+') AS DECIMAL)) AS total_duration_seconds,
  -- Alternative approach using milliseconds column
  SUM(milliseconds)/1000.0 AS total_duration_seconds_from_ms
FROM pitstops
WHERE raceid = 842
GROUP BY raceid;

SELECT 
    label,
    JSON_AGG(
        JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
            'molecule_id', m.molecule_id,
            'atoms', (
                SELECT JSON_AGG(JSON_BUILD_OBJECT('atom_id', a.atom_id, 'element', a.element))
                FROM atom a
                WHERE a.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
            ),
            'bonds', (
                SELECT JSON_AGG(JSON_BUILD_OBJECT('bond_id', b.bond_id, 'bond_type', b.bond_type))
                FROM bond b
                WHERE b.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
            )
        )
    ) AS grouped_molecules
FROM 
    molecule m
GROUP BY 
    label

SELECT 
    molecule_id, 
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN element = 'na' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS na_atoms,
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN element = 'c' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
         AND SUM(CASE WHEN element = 'cl' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
        THEN 1
        ELSE COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN element IN ('c', 'cl') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0)
    END AS c_cl_atoms
FROM atom
GROUP BY molecule_id;

SELECT 
    home_team_api_id, 
    CAST(SUBSTR(date, 12, 2) AS INTEGER) AS hour_of_day,
    AVG(home_team_goal) as avg_home_goals, 
    AVG(away_team_goal) as avg_away_goals, 
    SUM(home_team_goal) as total_home_goals, 
    SUM(away_team_goal) as total_away_goals, 
    MAX(home_team_goal) as max_home_goals, 
    MIN(home_team_goal) as min_home_goals, 
    COUNT(home_team_api_id) as count 
FROM match 
GROUP BY home_team_api_id, CAST(SUBSTR(date, 12, 2) AS INTEGER)
ORDER BY home_team_api_id, hour_of_day;

WITH numbered_transactions AS (
  SELECT 
    transaction_date,
    club_name,
    amount,
    sign(amount) AS amount_sign,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY club_name ORDER BY transaction_date) - 
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY club_name, sign(amount) ORDER BY transaction_date) AS grp
  FROM club_transactions
),
grouped_transactions AS (
  SELECT 
    MIN(transaction_date) AS start_date,
    MAX(transaction_date) AS end_date,
    club_name,
    amount_sign,
    SUM(amount) AS total_amount
  FROM numbered_transactions
  GROUP BY club_name, amount_sign, grp
  ORDER BY club_name, start_date
)
SELECT 
  start_date,
  CASE WHEN start_date <> end_date THEN end_date ELSE NULL END AS end_date,
  club_name,
  total_amount
FROM grouped_transactions
ORDER BY club_name, start_date

                    c.module_code,
                    c.name_module,
                    c.id_parent_module
                FROM modules c

    WHERE p.id_parent_module IS NULL

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_gap_fill_update(tbl text, id_col text, order_col text) 
RETURNS void LANGUAGE plpgsql AS 
$$
DECLARE 
    col text;
    nullable_cols text[];
    update_stmt text;
BEGIN
    -- Get all nullable columns from the table
    SELECT array_agg(column_name::text)
    INTO nullable_cols
    FROM information_schema.columns 
    WHERE table_name = tbl 
    AND is_nullable = 'YES';
    
    -- For each nullable column, create and execute an update statement with last_value window function
    FOREACH col IN ARRAY nullable_cols
    LOOP
        update_stmt := format('
            UPDATE %I t1
            SET %I = t2.last_non_null_value
            FROM (
                SELECT 
                    %I as row_id,
                    %I,
                    LAST_VALUE(%I IGNORE NULLS) OVER (
                        PARTITION BY %I 
                        ORDER BY %I 
                        ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
                    ) as last_non_null_value
                FROM %I
            ) t2
            WHERE t1.%I = t2.row_id
            AND t1.%I IS NULL
            AND t2.last_non_null_value IS NOT NULL',
            tbl, col, id_col, id_col, col, id_col, order_col, tbl, id_col, col);
            
        EXECUTE update_stmt;
    END LOOP;
END;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.generate_series_monthly(a date, b date)
RETURNS SETOF date 
LANGUAGE SQL 
IMMUTABLE 
PARALLEL SAFE 
ROWS 12 AS 
$function$
  SELECT generate_series(date_trunc('month', a), date_trunc('month', b), '1 month')
$function$;

-- Test the row estimate with direct function call
EXPLAIN SELECT generate_series_monthly('2024-01-01', '2024-05-01');

-- Test the row estimate when used in FROM clause
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM generate_series_monthly('2024-01-01', '2024-05-01');

WITH matches_with_draw_markers AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        date,
        home_team_goal,
        away_team_goal,
        home_team_goal + away_team_goal AS total_goals,
        CASE WHEN home_team_goal = away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_draw,
        SUM(CASE WHEN home_team_goal = away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
            OVER (ORDER BY id, date) AS draw_group
    FROM match
),
goals_between_draws AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        date,
        home_team_goal,
        away_team_goal,
        is_draw,
        draw_group,
        SUM(total_goals) OVER (
            PARTITION BY draw_group
            ORDER BY id, date
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
        ) AS running_total_goals
    FROM matches_with_draw_markers
)
SELECT 
    id,
    date,
    home_team_goal,
    away_team_goal,
    running_total_goals AS amount
FROM goals_between_draws
WHERE is_draw = 1
ORDER BY id, date;

WITH LastTransPerStation AS (
    -- Get the last transaction for each customer-gasstation combination
    SELECT *
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            t.*,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC) as rn
        FROM transactions_1k t
    ) ranked
    WHERE rn = 1
),
CustomerIssueStations AS (
    -- For each customer, find the first gas station with amount < 10 (chronologically)
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        FIRST_VALUE(gasstationid) OVER (
            PARTITION BY customerid 
            ORDER BY date, time
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
        ) AS first_issue_station
    FROM LastTransPerStation
    WHERE amount < 10
),
LastNormalStation AS (
    -- For each customer, find the last gas station with amount >= 10
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        LAST_VALUE(gasstationid) OVER (
            PARTITION BY customerid 
            ORDER BY date, time
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
        ) AS last_normal_station
    FROM LastTransPerStation
    WHERE amount >= 10
)
SELECT 
    t.customerid,
    t.transactionid,
    t.gasstationid,
    t.amount
FROM LastTransPerStation t
LEFT JOIN CustomerIssueStations i ON t.customerid = i.customerid
LEFT JOIN LastNormalStation n ON t.customerid = n.customerid
WHERE 
    -- Include transaction if it's from the first station with an issue
    (i.first_issue_station = t.gasstationid)
    OR 
    -- Or if there's no issue station and it's the last normal station
    (i.first_issue_station IS NULL AND t.gasstationid = n.last_normal_station);

WITH login_sessions AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        action_time AS login_time,
        LEAD(action_time) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY action_time) AS next_login_time
    FROM user_actions
    WHERE action = 'LOGIN'
),
search_actions AS (
    SELECT 
        s.user_id,
        s.action_time AS search_time,
        l.login_time,
        l.next_login_time
    FROM user_actions s
    JOIN login_sessions l ON s.user_id = l.user_id
        AND s.action_time > l.login_time
        AND (l.next_login_time IS NULL OR s.action_time < l.next_login_time)
    WHERE s.action = 'SEARCH'
),
buy_actions AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        action_time AS buy_time
    FROM user_actions
    WHERE action = 'BUY'
)
SELECT 
    s.user_id,
    COUNT(*) AS abandoned_searches
FROM search_actions s
LEFT JOIN buy_actions b ON s.user_id = b.user_id
    AND b.buy_time > s.search_time
    AND (s.next_login_time IS NULL OR b.buy_time < s.next_login_time)
WHERE b.user_id IS NULL
GROUP BY s.user_id;

SELECT 
    player_id,
    MAX(CASE WHEN pos = 1 AND key = 'goals' THEN value ELSE NULL END) AS goals,
    MAX(CASE WHEN pos = 1 AND key = 'assists' THEN value ELSE NULL END) AS assists,
    MAX(CASE WHEN pos = 1 AND key = 'yellow_cards' THEN value ELSE NULL END) AS yellow_cards
FROM (
    SELECT 
        player_id,
        TRIM(UNNEST(STRING_TO_ARRAY(stats_keys, ','))) AS key,
        TRIM(UNNEST(STRING_TO_ARRAY(stats_values, ','))) AS value,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY player_id, TRIM(UNNEST(STRING_TO_ARRAY(stats_keys, ','))) ORDER BY player_id) AS pos
    FROM player_stats
) AS expanded
GROUP BY player_id
ORDER BY player_id;

SELECT 
    begin_date, 
    end_date,
    CASE 
        -- If begin_date is the first day of a month and end_date is the last day of a month
        WHEN (EXTRACT(DAY FROM begin_date) = 1 AND 
              EXTRACT(DAY FROM end_date) = EXTRACT(DAY FROM (DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')))
        THEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(end_date, begin_date)) * 12 + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(end_date, begin_date))
        
        -- If begin_date is the first day of a month but end_date is not the last day of a month
        WHEN (EXTRACT(DAY FROM begin_date) = 1)
        THEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date), begin_date)) * 12 + 
             EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date), begin_date))
        
        -- If begin_date is not the first day of a month but end_date is the last day of a month
        WHEN (EXTRACT(DAY FROM end_date) = EXTRACT(DAY FROM (DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')))
        THEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(end_date, DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month')) * 12 + 
             EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(end_date, DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month'))
        
        -- If neither begin_date is the first day nor end_date is the last day of their respective months
        ELSE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date), DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month')) * 12 + 
             EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date), DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month'))
    END AS full_calendar_months
FROM race_dates;

WITH height_changes AS (
  SELECT 
    entity_id,
    updated_at,
    value AS height_value
  FROM player_stats_changes
  WHERE column_id = 'height'
),
weight_changes AS (
  SELECT 
    entity_id,
    updated_at,
    value AS weight_value
  FROM player_stats_changes
  WHERE column_id = 'weight'
),
all_changes AS (
  SELECT 
    COALESCE(h.entity_id, w.entity_id) AS entity_id,
    COALESCE(h.updated_at, w.updated_at) AS updated_at,
    h.height_value,
    w.weight_value
  FROM height_changes h
  FULL OUTER JOIN weight_changes w 
    ON h.entity_id = w.entity_id AND h.updated_at = w.updated_at
),
changes_with_previous AS (
  SELECT 
    entity_id,
    updated_at,
    height_value,
    weight_value,
    LAST_VALUE(height_value) IGNORE NULLS OVER (
      PARTITION BY entity_id 
      ORDER BY updated_at 
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS current_height,
    LAST_VALUE(weight_value) IGNORE NULLS OVER (
      PARTITION BY entity_id 
      ORDER BY updated_at 
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS current_weight
  FROM all_changes
)
SELECT 
  entity_id,
  updated_at,
  current_height AS height,
  current_weight AS weight
FROM changes_with_previous
ORDER BY entity_id, updated_at;

WITH combined_data AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup1, subgroup2
    FROM superhero_group
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL OR subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
group_mapping AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        COALESCE(subgroup1, subgroup2) AS group_identifier
    FROM combined_data
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL OR subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT 
        id,
        subgroup2 AS group_identifier
    FROM combined_data
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL AND subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
group_clusters AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        subgroup1,
        subgroup2,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY group_identifier ORDER BY group_identifier) AS group_id
    FROM combined_data
    JOIN group_mapping USING (id)
)
SELECT 
    id AS item,
    subgroup1,
    subgroup2,
    group_id AS group
FROM group_clusters
ORDER BY item;

SELECT 
    u.row_id,
    u.utm_campaign,
    u.utm_source,
    u.utm_medium,
    u.utm_content,
    u.utm_term,
    r.date_of_visit,
    SUM(r.sessions) AS total_sessions,
    SUM(r.pageviews) AS total_pageviews,
    SUM(r.bounces) AS total_bounces
FROM 
    utm u
LEFT JOIN 
    report r ON 
    u.row_id = r.id AND
    (u.utm_campaign = r.utm_campaign OR (u.utm_campaign IS NULL AND r.utm_campaign IS NULL)) AND
    (u.utm_source = r.utm_source OR (u.utm_source IS NULL AND r.utm_source IS NULL)) AND
    (u.utm_medium = r.utm_medium OR (u.utm_medium IS NULL AND r.utm_medium IS NULL))
GROUP BY 
    u.row_id,
    u.utm_campaign,
    u.utm_source,
    u.utm_medium,
    u.utm_content,
    u.utm_term,
    r.date_of_visit
ORDER BY 
    u.row_id, 
    r.date_of_visit;

SELECT
    id,
    name,
    text,
    ARRAY(
        SELECT regexp_matches[1]
        FROM regexp_matches(text, '\{\{[^}]+\}\}', 'g') AS regexp_matches
    ) AS bracketed_tokens,
    ARRAY_LENGTH(
        ARRAY(
            SELECT regexp_matches[1]
            FROM regexp_matches(text, '\{\{[^}]+\}\}', 'g') AS regexp_matches
        ),
        1
    ) AS token_count
FROM cards
WHERE text IS NOT NULL AND text ~ '\{\{[^}]+\}\}';

WITH race_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        gender_id,
        race_id,
        COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM 
        superhero
    GROUP BY 
        gender_id, race_id
),
ranked_races AS (
    SELECT 
        gender_id,
        race_id,
        cnt,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY cnt DESC) AS top_rank,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY cnt ASC) AS bottom_rank
    FROM 
        race_counts
)
SELECT 
    gender_id,
    race_id,
    cnt,
    'top' AS rank_type
FROM 
    ranked_races
WHERE 
    top_rank <= 5

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    gender_id,
    race_id,
    cnt,
    'bottom' AS rank_type
FROM 
    ranked_races
WHERE 
    bottom_rank <= 5
ORDER BY 
    gender_id, 
    rank_type, 
    CASE WHEN rank_type = 'top' THEN cnt END DESC,
    CASE WHEN rank_type = 'bottom' THEN cnt END ASC;

        '56f4935b-f6c5-59b9-88bf-9bcce20247ce', 
        '8dfc67e9-8323-5d1f-9e25-9f9394abd5a0', 
        '5ac794d2-4c66-5332-afb1-54b24bc11823', 
        '60f49caf-3583-5f85-b4b3-08dca73a8628'
    )
)
SELECT

ORDER BY ruling_count DESC, uuid, date DESC;

WITH driver_totals AS (
    SELECT id, name, total_points,
           DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY total_points DESC) AS driver_rank
    FROM racers
    GROUP BY id, name, total_points
)
SELECT 
    dt.driver_rank AS place,
    r.id,
    r.name,
    r.total_points,
    r.race_points,
    r.raceid
FROM 
    racers r
JOIN 
    driver_totals dt ON r.id = dt.id AND r.total_points = dt.total_points

    dt.driver_rank, r.id, r.raceid;

WITH first_purchase AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MIN(month_year) AS first_month_year
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY customer_id
)

SELECT 
    o.month_year,
    COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) AS repeat_customers
FROM 
    orders o
JOIN 
    first_purchase fp ON o.customer_id = fp.customer_id
WHERE 
    o.month_year > fp.first_month_year
GROUP BY 
    o.month_year
ORDER BY 
    o.month_year

WITH ranked_events AS (
  SELECT 
    player_name,
    event,
    event_date,
    LEAD(event_date) OVER (PARTITION BY player_name ORDER BY event_date) AS next_event_date,
    LEAD(event) OVER (PARTITION BY player_name ORDER BY event_date) AS next_event
  FROM player_movements
),
join_periods AS (
  SELECT 
    player_name,
    event_date AS join_date,
    CASE 
      WHEN next_event = 'Leave' THEN next_event_date
      ELSE NULL
    END AS leave_date
  FROM ranked_events
  WHERE event = 'Join'
)
SELECT 
  player_name,
  join_date,
  leave_date
FROM join_periods
WHERE leave_date IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY player_name, join_date;

WITH view_pairs AS (
    SELECT 
        start_view.session_id,
        start_view.post_id,
        start_view.ts AS start_time,
        end_view.ts AS end_time,
        end_view.view_perc
    FROM 
        view_logs start_view
    JOIN 
        view_logs end_view 
        ON start_view.session_id = end_view.session_id 
        AND start_view.post_id = end_view.post_id
        AND start_view.event_name = 'view_start'
        AND end_view.event_name = 'view_end'
        AND start_view.ts < end_view.ts
    WHERE 
        NOT EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 
            FROM view_logs middle_view
            WHERE middle_view.session_id = start_view.session_id
            AND middle_view.post_id = start_view.post_id
            AND middle_view.ts > start_view.ts 
            AND middle_view.ts < end_view.ts
        )
)
SELECT 
    session_id,
    post_id,
    start_time,
    end_time,
    view_perc,
    CASE 
        WHEN (end_time - start_time > 3 AND view_perc > 0.8) THEN 'true' 
        ELSE 'false' 
    END AS has_impact
FROM 
    view_pairs
ORDER BY 
    session_id, 
    start_time;

WITH yesterday_data AS (
    SELECT SUM(amount) AS revenue
    FROM transactions
    WHERE timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 1
),
week_ago_data AS (
    SELECT SUM(amount) AS revenue
    FROM transactions
    WHERE timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 8
),
current_mtd AS (
    SELECT SUM(amount) AS revenue
    FROM transactions
    WHERE date_trunc('month', timestamp) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date)
    AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1
),
prev_mtd AS (
    SELECT SUM(amount) AS revenue
    FROM transactions
    WHERE date_trunc('month', timestamp) = date_trunc('month', '2022-01-27'::date - interval '1 month')
    AND date_part('day', timestamp) <= date_part('day', '2022-01-27'::date - 1)
)
SELECT 
    yesterday_data.revenue AS yesterday_revenue,
    ROUND(
        (yesterday_data.revenue - week_ago_data.revenue) / NULLIF(week_ago_data.revenue, 0)::numeric, 
        2
    ) AS pct_change_week_ago,
    current_mtd.revenue AS mtd,
    ROUND(
        (current_mtd.revenue - prev_mtd.revenue) / NULLIF(prev_mtd.revenue, 0)::numeric,
        2
    ) AS pct_change_month_prior
FROM 
    yesterday_data,
    week_ago_data,
    current_mtd,
    prev_mtd;

WITH high_score_parents AS (
    SELECT id
    FROM posts
    WHERE score >= 20
)
SELECT p.id, p.acceptedanswerid, p.posttypeid, p.score
FROM posts p
WHERE 
    -- Include parent posts with score >= 20
    (p.score >= 20 AND p.id NOT IN (
        SELECT acceptedanswerid 
        FROM posts 
        WHERE acceptedanswerid IS NOT NULL
    ))
    OR
    -- Include child posts where parent score < 20 but child score >= 20
    (p.score >= 20 AND p.posttypeid = 2 AND p.parentid NOT IN (SELECT id FROM high_score_parents))

WITH parent_posts AS (
    SELECT id, score
    FROM posts
    WHERE posttypeid = 1  -- Question posts
),
high_score_parents AS (
    SELECT id
    FROM parent_posts
    WHERE score >= 20
)
SELECT p.id, p.posttypeid, p.score, p.parentid
FROM posts p
WHERE 
    -- Include parent posts with score >= 20
    (p.posttypeid = 1 AND p.score >= 20)
    OR
    -- Include child posts where parent score < 20 but child score >= 20
    (p.score >= 20 AND p.posttypeid = 2 AND p.parentid IN (
        SELECT id FROM parent_posts WHERE score < 20
    ))
    AND
    -- Exclude child posts whose parents are already included
    (p.parentid NOT IN (SELECT id FROM high_score_parents))

WITH card_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        to_date(fe.start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS start_date,
        to_date(fe.end_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS end_date,
        dp.set_name,
        dp.set_code
    FROM fact_collection fe
    INNER JOIN dim_set dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
month_series AS (
    SELECT generate_series(
        date_trunc('month', (SELECT MIN(to_date(start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD')) FROM fact_collection)),
        date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE),
        INTERVAL '1 month'
    ) AS month_date
)
SELECT 
    ms.month_date AS month,
    COUNT(ca.set_code) AS existing_cards,
    ca.set_name
FROM month_series ms
LEFT JOIN card_activity ca ON 
    ca.start_date < (ms.month_date + INTERVAL '1 month') AND
    (ca.end_date IS NULL OR ca.end_date >= ms.month_date)
GROUP BY 
    ms.month_date,
    ca.set_name
ORDER BY 
    ms.month_date,
    ca.set_name;

WITH numbered_missions AS (
  SELECT 
    hero_id, 
    mission_start, 
    mission_end,
    SUM(CASE 
          WHEN LAG(mission_end) OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) = mission_start 
          THEN 0 
          ELSE 1 
        END) OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) AS span_group
  FROM superhero_missions
),
grouped_missions AS (
  SELECT 
    hero_id, 
    span_group,
    MIN(mission_start) AS span_start,
    MAX(mission_end) AS span_end
  FROM numbered_missions
  GROUP BY hero_id, span_group
)
SELECT 
  hero_id, 
  span_start AS mission_start, 
  span_end AS mission_end
FROM grouped_missions
ORDER BY hero_id, span_start;

WITH RECURSIVE league_hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: select all leagues
    SELECT id AS league_id, name, country_id
    FROM league
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: This would join child leagues to parent leagues
    -- But since there's no parent-child relationship in your schema,
    -- this part won't actually do anything in your current database structure
    SELECT l.id AS league_id, l.name, l.country_id
    FROM league l
    JOIN league_hierarchy lh ON l.id = lh.league_id
    WHERE 1=0  -- This prevents infinite recursion since there's no actual hierarchy
)
-- Get all teams that participate in these leagues
SELECT DISTINCT t.team_api_id, t.team_long_name, l.name AS league_name
FROM league_hierarchy l
JOIN match m ON l.league_id = m.league_id
JOIN team t ON m.home_team_api_id = t.team_api_id OR m.away_team_api_id = t.team_api_id
ORDER BY l.name, t.team_long_name;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION trigger_function() RETURNS TRIGGER AS 
$BODY$
BEGIN
    INSERT INTO public.destination_table (
        created_at, 
        match_id, 
        player_id, 
        event_type, 
        url, 
        user_id
    )
    VALUES (
        NEW.created_at,
        NEW.match_id,
        NEW.player_id,
        NEW.event_type,
        split_part(NEW.url, '?', 1),
        (NEW.data->'local_storage'->'data'->>'user_id')::varchar
    );
    
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$BODY$ 
LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER after_insert_source
AFTER INSERT ON public.source_table
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE PROCEDURE trigger_function();

    VALUES

    (2, 2, 90, 90, 85, 90, 88, 90),

), 
score_check AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        max_score,
        year_1,

        year_3,
        year_4,

                 (year_1 < max_score) OR (year_2 < max_score) OR (year_3 < max_score) OR

            THEN TRUE

WITH RECURSIVE item_costs AS (
    -- Base case: direct resource costs for each item
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        MAX(item_price) AS retail_price,
        SUM(CASE WHEN resource_id IS NOT NULL THEN resource_price * quantity ELSE 0 END) AS partial_craft_price,
        SUM(CASE WHEN resource_id IS NOT NULL THEN resource_price * quantity ELSE 0 END) AS initial_craft_cost,
        0 AS level
    FROM store
    GROUP BY item_id
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: add costs of component items
    SELECT 
        ic.item_id,
        ic.retail_price,
        ic.partial_craft_price,
        ic.initial_craft_cost + COALESCE(s.uses_item_price * s.quantity, 0),
        ic.level + 1
    FROM item_costs ic
    JOIN store s ON ic.item_id = s.item_id
    WHERE s.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL AND ic.level < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
)

SELECT 
    item_id, 
    retail_price,
    partial_craft_price,
    MAX(initial_craft_cost) AS full_craft_price
FROM item_costs
GROUP BY item_id, retail_price, partial_craft_price
ORDER BY item_id;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION reindex_superhero_ids(publisher_id_param BIGINT) 
RETURNS VOID 
LANGUAGE plpgsql AS 
$$
DECLARE
    seq_val BIGINT;
BEGIN
    -- Create a temporary table to store the mapping
    CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_id_mapping AS
    SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS new_id
    FROM superhero
    WHERE publisher_id = publisher_id_param;
    
    -- First update to temporary high values to avoid conflicts
    UPDATE superhero
    SET hair_colour_id = hair_colour_id + 2000
    WHERE publisher_id = publisher_id_param;
    
    -- Then update to new sequential values
    UPDATE superhero s
    SET hair_colour_id = m.new_id
    FROM temp_id_mapping m
    WHERE s.id = m.id AND s.publisher_id = publisher_id_param;
    
    -- Drop the temporary table
    DROP TABLE temp_id_mapping;
END;
$$;

WITH RECURSIVE atom_ancestors AS (
    -- Base case: start with the specified atom
    SELECT atom_id, parent_id, enabled
    FROM connection
    WHERE atom_id = 4  -- Assuming we're looking for atom with ID 4
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: find all ancestors
    SELECT c.atom_id, c.parent_id, c.enabled
    FROM connection c
    INNER JOIN atom_ancestors aa ON c.atom_id = aa.parent_id
    WHERE aa.enabled = true  -- Only continue if the child is enabled
)
-- Only return results if ALL ancestors are enabled
SELECT *
FROM atom_ancestors
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM atom_ancestors
    WHERE enabled = false
)

WITH hero_attribute_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        ha.hero_id,
        ha.attribute_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT ha.attribute_value) AS distinct_value_count
    FROM 
        hero_attribute ha
    GROUP BY 
        ha.hero_id, ha.attribute_id
    HAVING 
        COUNT(DISTINCT ha.attribute_value) > 1
)
SELECT 
    s.id AS hero_id,
    s.superhero_name,
    a.attribute_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ha.attribute_value) AS different_values_count,
    STRING_AGG(CAST(ha.attribute_value AS TEXT), ', ' ORDER BY ha.attribute_value) AS values
FROM 
    hero_attribute_counts hac
JOIN 
    superhero s ON hac.hero_id = s.id
JOIN 
    attribute a ON hac.attribute_id = a.id
JOIN 
    hero_attribute ha ON ha.hero_id = hac.hero_id AND ha.attribute_id = hac.attribute_id
GROUP BY 
    s.id, s.superhero_name, a.attribute_name
ORDER BY 
    s.superhero_name, a.attribute_name;

WITH event_dates AS (
  -- Get all dates for the specified event
  SELECT 
    CAST(DATETIME(event_date, 'UTC') AT TIME ZONE 'MST' AS DATE) AS event_day
  FROM event
  WHERE event_name = 'April Meeting'
),
numbered_dates AS (
  -- Number the dates and calculate the difference to identify gaps
  SELECT 
    event_day,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY event_day) AS row_num,
    DATE(event_day, '-' || ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY event_day) || ' days') AS date_group
  FROM event_dates
),
streaks AS (
  -- Group by the date_group to identify consecutive dates
  SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS streak_count,
    MIN(event_day) AS start_date,
    MAX(event_day) AS end_date,
    date_group
  FROM numbered_dates
  GROUP BY date_group
  ORDER BY end_date DESC
)
-- Get the most recent streak
SELECT 
  streak_count,
  'April Meeting' AS event_name,
  'MST' AS timezone,
  start_date,
  end_date
FROM streaks
ORDER BY end_date DESC
LIMIT 1;

WITH max_date_values AS (
    SELECT id, date, got, gpt
    FROM laboratory
    WHERE date = (SELECT MAX(date) FROM laboratory l2 WHERE l2.id = laboratory.id)
),
min_date_values AS (
    SELECT id, date, got, gpt
    FROM laboratory
    WHERE date = (SELECT MIN(date) FROM laboratory l2 WHERE l2.id = laboratory.id)
)
SELECT m.id, 
       min_date_values.date AS min_date, 
       m.date AS max_date, 
       m.got, 
       m.gpt
FROM max_date_values m
JOIN min_date_values ON m.id = min_date_values.id
WHERE m.got = min_date_values.got 
  AND m.gpt = min_date_values.gpt
ORDER BY m.id;

WITH constructor_roles AS (
    SELECT 18 race_id, 1 constructor_id, false customer_billing, true commander, true agent 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 18, 2, true, false, false
)
SELECT 
    n.race_id,
    string_agg(distinct role, ', ') AS finance
FROM constructor_roles n
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT 'AR (Customer Billing)' AS role WHERE n.customer_billing = TRUE
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'AP (Commander)' AS role WHERE n.commander = TRUE
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'AP (Agent)' AS role WHERE n.agent = TRUE
) roles
WHERE n.race_id = 18
GROUP BY n.race_id;

SELECT 
    s.firstName, 
    (
        SELECT jsonb_agg(DISTINCT year_data)
        FROM (
            SELECT 
                y.id AS yearId,
                (
                    SELECT jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object('classId', c.id))
                    FROM classes c
                    JOIN classYears cy ON c.id = cy.classId
                    JOIN classYearStudents cys2 ON cy.id = cys2.classYearId
                    WHERE cy.yearId = y.id
                    AND cys2.studentId = s.id
                ) AS classes
            FROM years y
            JOIN classYears cy ON y.id = cy.yearId
            JOIN classYearStudents cys ON cy.id = cys.classYearId
            WHERE cys.studentId = s.id
        ) AS year_data
    ) AS years
FROM users3 s
ORDER BY s.id;

WITH RECURSIVE hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: start with all employees
    SELECT 
        id AS employee_id,
        id AS current_id,
        bossid AS current_boss_id,
        salary AS employee_salary,
        salary AS current_salary,
        0 AS hierarchical_level
    FROM staff
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: move up the hierarchy
    SELECT 
        h.employee_id,
        s.id AS current_id,
        s.bossid AS current_boss_id,
        h.employee_salary,
        s.salary AS current_salary,
        h.hierarchical_level + 1
    FROM hierarchy h
    JOIN staff s ON h.current_boss_id = s.id
    WHERE h.current_boss_id IS NOT NULL
)

-- Find the lowest-ranked indirect manager with at least twice the salary
SELECT DISTINCT ON (employee_id)
    employee_id,
    employee_salary,
    hierarchical_level,
    current_id AS boss_id,
    current_salary AS boss_salary
FROM hierarchy
WHERE 
    hierarchical_level > 0 -- Indirect manager (not direct)
    AND current_salary >= employee_salary * 2 -- At least twice the salary
ORDER BY 
    employee_id, 
    hierarchical_level ASC -- Get the lowest-ranked (closest) manager first

WITH ranked_cards AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    name,
    rarity,
    convertedmanacost,
    edhrecrank,
    multiverseid,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY rarity 
      ORDER BY convertedmanacost DESC NULLS LAST, 
               edhrecrank DESC NULLS LAST, 
               multiverseid ASC NULLS LAST
    ) as rn
  FROM cards
  WHERE rarity IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
  rarity,
  id,
  name,
  convertedmanacost,
  edhrecrank,
  multiverseid
FROM ranked_cards
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY rarity;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS card, disp, account;

-- First create account table without the disp_id reference
CREATE TABLE account (
    account_id INT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    district_id INT NOT NULL,
    frequency TEXT NOT NULL,
    date DATE NOT NULL
);

-- Then create disp table with reference to account
CREATE TABLE disp (
    disp_id INT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    client_id INT NOT NULL,
    account_id INT NOT NULL,
    type TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES account(account_id) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE
);

-- Create card table with reference to disp
CREATE TABLE card (
    card_id INT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    disp_id INT NOT NULL,
    type TEXT NOT NULL,
    issued DATE NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (disp_id) REFERENCES disp(disp_id) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE
);

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id, 
        amount AS cum_sum, 
        1 AS bucket
    FROM transfer 
    WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM transfer)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        t.id,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN t.amount 
            ELSE c.cum_sum + t.amount 
        END AS cum_sum,
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN c.bucket + 1 
            ELSE c.bucket 
        END AS bucket
    FROM cte c
    JOIN transfer t ON t.id = c.id + 1
)
SELECT id, cum_sum, bucket
FROM cte
ORDER BY id;

SELECT

        SELECT laps
        FROM results r2
        WHERE r2.raceid = r1.raceid

    ) AS modal_laps

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN t1.table_name IS NULL THEN 'extra'
        WHEN t2.table_name IS NULL THEN 'missing'
    END AS kind,
    COALESCE(t1.table_name, t2.table_name) AS table_name
FROM 
    required_tables t1
FULL OUTER JOIN 
    (SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public') t2
ON 
    t1.table_name = t2.table_name
WHERE 
    t1.table_name IS NULL OR t2.table_name IS NULL
ORDER BY 
    kind, table_name;

SELECT SUM(attribute_value) AS sum

WITH pattern_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT("ANA Pattern") AS count,
        "ANA Pattern",
        EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "Examination Date") AS examination_year,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "Examination Date") ORDER BY COUNT("ANA Pattern") DESC) AS rank
    FROM examination 
    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "Examination Date") BETWEEN 1993 AND 1996
    AND "ANA Pattern" IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "Examination Date"), "ANA Pattern"

WITH player_names AS (
    SELECT 1 AS source, 
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY player_name) AS row_number, 
           CONCAT(player_name, ' (', LEFT(birthday, 4), ')') AS dest 
    FROM player_table
), 
birth_year_summary AS (
    SELECT 2 AS source, 
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(*), LEFT(birthday, 4)) AS row_number, 
           CONCAT('There are a total of ', COUNT(*), ' player', 
                 CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 1 THEN 's' ELSE '' END, 
                 ' born in ', LEFT(birthday, 4), '.') AS dest 
    FROM player_table 
    GROUP BY LEFT(birthday, 4)
) 
SELECT dest 
FROM (
    SELECT * FROM player_names 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT * FROM birth_year_summary
) AS combined_results 
ORDER BY source, row_number;

SELECT 
    rs.raceId AS race_id,

LEFT JOIN (

    FROM 
        results
    GROUP BY 
        raceId
) c ON rs.raceId = c.raceId
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        raceId,
        string_agg(DISTINCT stop::TEXT, ', ' ORDER BY stop) AS stops
    FROM

    GROUP BY

) p ON rs.raceId = p.raceId
ORDER BY

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Update the eff_to date for the previous record of the same team
    UPDATE team_attributes
    SET eff_to = TO_TIMESTAMP(NEW.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE - INTERVAL '1 day'
    WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
      AND eff_to IS NULL
      AND TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE < TO_TIMESTAMP(NEW.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE;
    
    -- Update all records for this team to ensure proper sequencing
    UPDATE team_attributes ta
    SET eff_to = subquery.next_date - INTERVAL '1 day'
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            id,
            COALESCE(
                LEAD(TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE, 1) 
                OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE),
                TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ) AS next_date
        FROM team_attributes
        WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
    ) AS subquery
    WHERE ta.id = subquery.id
      AND ta.team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id;
    
    RETURN NULL;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes
AFTER INSERT ON team_attributes
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to();

WITH position_groups AS (
  SELECT 
    position,
    driverid,
    MAX(driverid) OVER (PARTITION BY position) as max_driverid
  FROM results
  WHERE position IS NOT NULL
),
position_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    position,
    COUNT(DISTINCT driverid) as driver_count
  FROM results
  WHERE position IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY position
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT driverid) > 1
)
SELECT DISTINCT pg.driverid
FROM position_groups pg
JOIN position_counts pc ON pg.position = pc.position
WHERE pg.driverid < pg.max_driverid
ORDER BY pg.driverid;

WITH RECURSIVE tree_with_colors AS (
    -- Base case: Start with leaf nodes that already have colors
    SELECT 
        id, 
        parent_id, 
        color
    FROM 
        superhero2
    WHERE 
        color IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Process parent nodes based on their children's colors
    SELECT 
        s.id, 
        s.parent_id,
        CASE 
            -- Count children with distinct colors
            WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT t.color) = 1 THEN MAX(t.color) -- If all children have same color, use that color
            ELSE 'gray' -- If children have different colors, use gray
        END AS color
    FROM 
        superhero2 s
    JOIN 
        tree_with_colors t ON s.id = t.parent_id
    WHERE 
        s.color IS NULL
    GROUP BY 
        s.id, s.parent_id
)
UPDATE superhero2
SET color = t.color
FROM tree_with_colors t
WHERE superhero2.id = t.id
AND (superhero2.color IS NULL OR superhero2.color <> t.color);

WITH artist_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        artist, 
        COUNT(*) AS total_cards
    FROM 
        cards
    WHERE 
        artist IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        artist
    HAVING 
        COUNT(*) > 1
),
artist_sets AS (
    SELECT 
        c.artist, 
        c.setcode, 
        COUNT(*) AS cards_in_set,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.artist ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS set_rank
    FROM 
        cards c
    JOIN 
        artist_totals at ON c.artist = at.artist
    GROUP BY 
        c.artist, c.setcode
)
SELECT 
    as1.artist,
    at.total_cards AS total_cards_by_artist,
    as1.setcode,
    as1.cards_in_set,
    CASE WHEN as1.set_rank = 1 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS is_top_set
FROM 
    artist_sets as1
JOIN 
    artist_totals at ON as1.artist = at.artist
ORDER BY 
    at.total_cards DESC,
    as1.artist,
    as1.cards_in_set DESC;

WITH recursive_chunks AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 50
            ELSE attribute_value
        END AS attribute_chunk,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS is_bifurcated,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN attribute_value - 50
            ELSE 0
        END AS remaining
    FROM hero_attribute
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN remaining > 50 THEN 50
            ELSE remaining
        END AS attribute_chunk,
        1 AS is_bifurcated,
        CASE 
            WHEN remaining > 50 THEN remaining - 50
            ELSE 0
        END AS remaining
    FROM recursive_chunks
    WHERE remaining > 0
)
INSERT INTO hero_attribute_bifurcation (hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_chunk, is_bifurcated)
SELECT 
    hero_id,
    attribute_id,
    attribute_chunk,
    is_bifurcated
FROM recursive_chunks
WHERE attribute_chunk > 0
ORDER BY hero_id, attribute_id;

SELECT a.district_id,

FROM account a
JOIN loan l ON a.account_id = l.account_id
GROUP BY a.district_id
ORDER BY a.district_id;

WITH activity_boundaries AS (
  SELECT 
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    ts,
    LEAD(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) as next_ts,
    CASE 
      WHEN ts - LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) > INTERVAL '5 minutes' 
      THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END as is_new_session
  FROM superhero_activities
),
activity_sessions AS (
  SELECT
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    ts,
    next_ts,
    SUM(is_new_session) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) as session_id
  FROM activity_boundaries
)
SELECT
  superhero_id,
  activity_code,
  MIN(ts) as start_time,
  MAX(next_ts) as end_time,
  MAX(next_ts) - MIN(ts) as duration
FROM activity_sessions
GROUP BY superhero_id, activity_code, session_id
ORDER BY superhero_id, activity_code, start_time;

WITH RECURSIVE card_hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: Select all root nodes (cards with no parent)
    SELECT
        uuid,
        card_name,
        parent_uuid,
        0 AS level
    FROM card_type
    WHERE parent_uuid IS NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Select all child nodes
    SELECT
        ct.uuid,
        ct.card_name,
        ct.parent_uuid,
        ch.level + 1
    FROM card_type ct
    JOIN card_hierarchy ch ON ct.parent_uuid = ch.uuid
)
-- Final query to structure the results
SELECT
    p.uuid AS parent_uuid,
    p.card_name AS parent_name,
    jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
            'uuid', c.uuid,
            'card_name', c.card_name
        )
    ) AS children
FROM card_hierarchy p
LEFT JOIN card_hierarchy c ON c.parent_uuid = p.uuid
WHERE p.parent_uuid IS NULL OR p.level > 0
GROUP BY p.uuid, p.card_name, p.level
ORDER BY p.level, p.card_name;

SELECT 
    d.the_day AS "Date", 
    COUNT(DISTINCT e.event_id) AS "Number of Events", 
    COALESCE(AVG(b.remaining), 0) AS "Avg Remaining Budget" 
FROM 
    (SELECT ts::date AS the_day 
     FROM generate_series(timestamp '2020-01-01', timestamp '2020-03-31', interval '1 day') ts) d 
LEFT JOIN 
    "event" e ON DATE(SUBSTR(e.event_date, 1, 10)) = d.the_day AND e.status IN ('Open', 'Closed')
LEFT JOIN 
    budget b ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id AND b.event_status IN ('Open', 'Closed')
GROUP BY 
    d.the_day 
ORDER BY 
    d.the_day;

SELECT 
    LPAD(EXTRACT(MONTH FROM trans.date)::text, 2, '0') AS month,
    SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM trans.date) = 1997 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) AS "1997",
    SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM trans.date) = 1998 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) AS "1998",
    SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM trans.date) = 1999 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) AS "1999"
FROM 
    trans
WHERE 
    trans.date >= '1997-01-01' AND trans.date < '2000-01-01'
GROUP BY 
    LPAD(EXTRACT(MONTH FROM trans.date)::text, 2, '0')
ORDER BY 
    month

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION words_get_chat(in_gid integer, in_social integer, in_sid text) 
RETURNS TABLE (out_mine integer, out_msg text) AS $func$
SELECT 
    CASE WHEN c.uid = s.uid THEN 1 ELSE 0 END, 
    c.msg 
FROM words_chat c 
JOIN words_games g USING (gid) 
JOIN words_social s ON s.uid IN (g.player1, g.player2) 
JOIN words_users u ON c.uid = u.uid
WHERE c.gid = in_gid 
  AND s.social = in_social 
  AND s.sid = in_sid 
  AND (
      -- Show all messages to the message author (even if they're muted)
      c.uid = s.uid 
      OR 
      -- Only show messages from non-muted users to others
      u.muted = false
  )
ORDER BY c.created ASC;
$func$ LANGUAGE sql;
SELECT words_get_chat(10, 100, 'abc') AS nice_user;

WITH driver_earnings AS (
    SELECT 
        driverid, 
        points, 
        (points * 100) AS earnings
    FROM 
        driverstandings
)
SELECT 
    driverid, 
    points, 
    earnings,
    CASE 
        WHEN earnings > 5000 THEN earnings * 0.2
        WHEN earnings > 3000 THEN earnings * 0.15
        ELSE 0
    END AS bonus,
    CASE 
        WHEN earnings > 5000 THEN earnings * 1.2
        WHEN earnings > 3000 THEN earnings * 1.15
        ELSE earnings
    END AS total_with_bonus
FROM 
    driver_earnings

WITH dataset AS (
  SELECT 
      date,
      COUNT(*) as rows_added
  FROM
      team_attributes
  WHERE 
      date BETWEEN '2010-01-01 00:00:00'
      AND '2015-12-31 00:00:00'
  GROUP BY 
      date
),
duplicate_dates AS (
  SELECT date
  FROM team_attributes
  WHERE date BETWEEN '2010-01-01 00:00:00' AND '2015-12-31 00:00:00'
  GROUP BY date
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS total_days_in_result_set,
    COUNT(DISTINCT rows_added) AS total_days_w_distinct_record_counts,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM duplicate_dates) AS total_days_w_duplicate_record_counts,
    STRING_AGG(DISTINCT dd.date, ', ') AS duplicate_dates
FROM dataset d
LEFT JOIN duplicate_dates dd ON 1=1

    FROM UNNEST(card_counters.counters) WITH ORDINALITY AS t1(e1, ord),
         UNNEST(ARRAY[2,0,2,1]) WITH ORDINALITY AS t2(e2, ord)

WITH RECURSIVE result_calculation AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id, 
        trx, 
        event, 
        ROUND((trx * event)::numeric, 6) AS result
    FROM 
        sales_transactions
    WHERE 
        id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM sales_transactions)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        s.id, 
        s.trx, 
        s.event, 
        ROUND((s.trx + r.result * s.event)::numeric, 6) AS result
    FROM 
        sales_transactions s
    JOIN 
        result_calculation r ON s.id = r.id + 1
)
SELECT 
    id, 
    result
FROM 
    result_calculation
ORDER BY 
    id;

WITH 
cte_funding_count AS (
    SELECT fundingtype, COUNT(*) AS count 
    FROM schools 
    GROUP BY fundingtype
),
cte_with_exact_percent AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype, 
        count,
        count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count) AS exact_percent,
        FLOOR(count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count)) AS floor_percent,
        count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count) - 
        FLOOR(count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count)) AS remainder
    FROM cte_funding_count
),
cte_total_floor AS (
    SELECT SUM(floor_percent) AS total_floor
    FROM cte_with_exact_percent
),
cte_with_rank AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype, 
        count, 
        floor_percent,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY remainder DESC) AS rank
    FROM cte_with_exact_percent
)
SELECT 
    fundingtype, 
    count,
    CASE 
        WHEN rank <= 100 - (SELECT total_floor FROM cte_total_floor) THEN floor_percent + 1
        ELSE floor_percent
    END AS percent
FROM cte_with_rank
ORDER BY count DESC;

SELECT pat.id, MIN(lab.date) AS firstActivity

WHERE pat.sex = 'F'

WITH filtered_keywords AS (
  SELECT 
    c.id,
    CASE 
      WHEN sq.removekeywords IS NULL THEN c.keywords
      ELSE array_remove_elements(c.keywords, sq.removekeywords)
    END AS filtered_keywords
  FROM 
    cards c
  JOIN 
    selectquery sq ON c.id = sq.id
  WHERE 
    c.id IN (61, 65)
)
SELECT 
  id,
  COALESCE(filtered_keywords, '{}') AS keywords
FROM 
  filtered_keywords
ORDER BY 
  id;

-- This query assumes you have a function array_remove_elements
-- If you don't have this function, here's an alternative approach:

WITH filtered_keywords AS (
  SELECT 
    c.id,
    array_agg(kw) FILTER (WHERE kw IS NOT NULL) AS filtered_keywords
  FROM 
    cards c
  JOIN 
    selectquery sq ON c.id = sq.id
  CROSS JOIN LATERAL unnest(c.keywords) AS kw
  WHERE 
    c.id IN (61, 65)
    AND kw <> ALL(SELECT unnest(sq.removekeywords))
  GROUP BY 
    c.id
)
SELECT 
  id,
  COALESCE(filtered_keywords, '{}') AS keywords
FROM 
  filtered_keywords
ORDER BY 
  id;

    AVG("C") AS c
FROM "Experiment"
WHERE "TIMESTAMP" >= '2021-01-07T00:00:00.000Z' 
  AND "TIMESTAMP" <= '2022-01-06T23:59:59.999Z'

WITH circuit_names AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    array_sort(array_remove(ARRAY[
      NULLIF(trim(circui_name_a1), ''),
      NULLIF(trim(circui_name_a2), ''),
      NULLIF(trim(circui_name_a3), '')
    ], NULL)) AS sorted_a_circuits,
    
    array_sort(array_remove(ARRAY[
      NULLIF(trim(circui_name_b1), ''),
      NULLIF(trim(circui_name_b2), ''),
      NULLIF(trim(circui_name_b3), '')
    ], NULL)) AS sorted_b_circuits,
    
    -- For display purposes
    string_agg(DISTINCT NULLIF(trim(circui_name_a1), ''), ', ') || 
    CASE WHEN NULLIF(trim(circui_name_a2), '') IS NOT NULL THEN ', ' || NULLIF(trim(circui_name_a2), '') ELSE '' END ||
    CASE WHEN NULLIF(trim(circui_name_a3), '') IS NOT NULL THEN ', ' || NULLIF(trim(circui_name_a3), '') ELSE '' END AS a_circuits,
    
    string_agg(DISTINCT NULLIF(trim(circui_name_b1), ''), ', ') || 
    CASE WHEN NULLIF(trim(circui_name_b2), '') IS NOT NULL THEN ', ' || NULLIF(trim(circui_name_b2), '') ELSE '' END ||
    CASE WHEN NULLIF(trim(circui_name_b3), '') IS NOT NULL THEN ', ' || NULLIF(trim(circui_name_b3), '') ELSE '' END AS b_circuits
  FROM 
    race_info
  GROUP BY 
    id, circui_name_a1, circui_name_a2, circui_name_a3, circui_name_b1, circui_name_b2, circui_name_b3
)
SELECT 
  id,
  a_circuits AS "Circuit Group A",
  b_circuits AS "Circuit Group B",
  sorted_a_circuits = sorted_b_circuits AS "Are Circuit Groups Identical",
  sorted_a_circuits,
  sorted_b_circuits
FROM 
  circuit_names
ORDER BY 
  id;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION array_sort(ANYARRAY)
RETURNS ANYARRAY AS $$
  SELECT ARRAY(SELECT unnest($1) ORDER BY 1)
$$ LANGUAGE SQL;

WITH trading_day_prices AS (
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        DATE(date_value) AS trading_day,
        MIN(date_value) AS first_price_time,
        (
            SELECT date_value
            FROM card_prices cp2
            WHERE cp2.card_id = cp1.card_id
            AND DATE(cp2.date_value) = DATE(cp1.date_value)
            AND cp2.date_value <= DATE_TRUNC('day', cp1.date_value) + INTERVAL '9 hours 15 minutes'
            ORDER BY cp2.date_value DESC
            LIMIT 1
        ) AS last_price_time_within_15min
    FROM card_prices cp1
    WHERE EXTRACT(HOUR FROM date_value) = 9
    AND EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM date_value) < 15
    GROUP BY card_id, DATE(date_value)
),
price_differences AS (
    SELECT 
        tdp.card_id,
        tdp.trading_day,
        (
            SELECT price
            FROM card_prices
            WHERE card_id = tdp.card_id
            AND date_value = tdp.last_price_time_within_15min
        ) - (
            SELECT price
            FROM card_prices
            WHERE card_id = tdp.card_id
            AND date_value = tdp.first_price_time
        ) AS price_difference
    FROM trading_day_prices tdp
    WHERE tdp.first_price_time IS NOT NULL
    AND tdp.last_price_time_within_15min IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    card_id,
    AVG(price_difference) AS avg_difference
FROM price_differences
GROUP BY card_id
ORDER BY card_id;

SELECT 
    b1.bond_id AS current_bond,
    b2.bond_id AS next_bond,
    b1.bond_type AS current_bond_type,
    b2.bond_type AS next_bond_type
FROM 
    bond b1
JOIN 
    connected c1 ON b1.bond_id = c1.bond_id
JOIN 
    connected c2 ON c1.atom_id2 = c2.atom_id
JOIN 
    bond b2 ON c2.bond_id = b2.bond_id
WHERE 
    b1.molecule_id = b2.molecule_id
    AND b1.bond_id != b2.bond_id
ORDER BY 
    b1.molecule_id, b1.bond_id, b2.bond_id;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION setup_vector_store(table_name text) 
RETURNS void 
LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $$ 
BEGIN
    -- Drop the function if it exists with the correct signature
    EXECUTE format('DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS match_%I(int, jsonb)', table_name);
    
    -- Create the table with vector support
    EXECUTE format('CREATE TABLE %I (
        id bigint PRIMARY KEY GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY,
        description text,
        metadata jsonb,
        embedding vector(1536)
    )', table_name);
    
    -- Create the matching function with dynamic name
    EXECUTE format('CREATE FUNCTION match_%I(
        match_count int DEFAULT NULL,
        filter jsonb DEFAULT ''{}'',
        query_embedding vector(1536)
    ) RETURNS TABLE (
        id bigint,
        description text,
        metadata jsonb,
        similarity float
    ) LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $func$
    BEGIN
        RETURN QUERY
        SELECT 
            t.id,
            t.description,
            t.metadata,
            1 - (t.embedding <=> query_embedding) AS similarity
        FROM %I t
        WHERE t.metadata @> filter
        ORDER BY t.embedding <=> query_embedding
        LIMIT match_count;
    END;
    $func$', table_name, table_name);
END;
$$;

WITH product_attributes AS (
  SELECT 
    dp.id AS datapoint_id,
    MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'ProductType' THEN vv.name END) AS product_type,
    MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'publishing_language' THEN vv.name END) AS language,
    MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'genre' THEN vv.name END) AS genre
  FROM datapoints dp
  JOIN datapoint_variablevalue dpvv ON dp.id = dpvv.datapoint_id
  JOIN variablevalue vv ON dpvv.variablevalue_id = vv.id
  JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id
  WHERE v.name IN ('ProductType', 'publishing_language', 'genre')
  GROUP BY dp.id
)

SELECT 
  td.years AS year,
  sd.name AS country,
  pa.product_type,
  pa.language,
  pa.genre,
  COALESCE(SUM(dp.value), 0) AS total_sales
FROM time_dimension td
CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
CROSS JOIN (
  SELECT DISTINCT product_type FROM product_attributes WHERE product_type IS NOT NULL
) pt
CROSS JOIN (
  SELECT DISTINCT language FROM product_attributes WHERE language IS NOT NULL
) l
CROSS JOIN (
  SELECT DISTINCT genre FROM product_attributes WHERE genre IS NOT NULL
) g
LEFT JOIN product_attributes pa ON 
  pa.product_type = pt.product_type AND
  pa.language = l.language AND
  pa.genre = g.genre
LEFT JOIN datapoints dp ON 
  dp.id = pa.datapoint_id AND
  dp.spatial_dimension_id = sd.id AND
  dp.time_dimension_id = td.id
GROUP BY td.years, sd.name, pa.product_type, pa.language, pa.genre
ORDER BY td.years DESC, sd.name, pa.product_type, pa.language, pa.genre;

FROM 
    information_schema.tables
WHERE 
    table_schema = 'public'
    AND table_type = 'BASE TABLE'
ORDER BY 
    pg_total_relation_size(quote_ident(table_name)) DESC
LIMIT 3;

WITH bond_times AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        SUM((bond_stat_->>'time')::numeric) AS total_time
    FROM 
        bond
    WHERE 
        bond_stat_->>'time' IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY 
        molecule_id
)
SELECT 
    b.bond_id,
    b.molecule_id,
    b.bond_type,
    (b.bond_stat_->>'time')::numeric AS bond_time
FROM 
    bond b
JOIN 
    bond_times bt ON b.molecule_id = bt.molecule_id
WHERE 
    b.bond_type = 'Unknown'
    AND (b.bond_stat_->>'time')::numeric > (0.1 * bt.total_time)
ORDER BY 
    b.molecule_id, b.bond_id;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.new_function(search_type text DEFAULT 'Unknown'::text) 
RETURNS SETOF bond 
LANGUAGE sql STABLE AS 
$function$ 
  SELECT * FROM bond 
  WHERE bond_stat_ @> jsonb_build_object('type', search_type)
     OR bond_stat_ @? ('$.** ? (@.type == "' || search_type || '")');
$function$;

WITH date_series AS (
  SELECT 
    b.id,
    b.rate,
    generate_series(
      LOWER(b.time_span)::date, 
      COALESCE(UPPER(b.time_span)::date, CURRENT_DATE), 
      '1 day'::interval
    )::date AS day_date
  FROM budgets b
),
month_ranges AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    day_date,
    DATE_TRUNC('month', day_date)::date AS month_start,
    (DATE_TRUNC('month', day_date) + INTERVAL '1 month')::date - 1 AS month_end,
    (DATE_TRUNC('month', day_date) + INTERVAL '1 month')::date - 
    DATE_TRUNC('month', day_date)::date AS days_in_month
  FROM date_series
)
SELECT 
  id,
  day_date,
  rate,
  days_in_month,
  ROUND((rate::numeric / days_in_month), 2) AS daily_rate
FROM month_ranges
ORDER BY id, day_date;

WITH range_values AS (
    SELECT 
        date_trunc('week', min(date)) as minval, 
        date_trunc('week', max(date)) as maxval 
    FROM laboratory
),
week_range AS (
    SELECT generate_series(minval, maxval, '1 week'::interval) as week 
    FROM range_values
),
grouped_labs AS (
    SELECT 
        date_trunc('week', date) as week, 
        count(id) as total_tests 
    FROM laboratory 
    GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT 
    week_range.week, 
    COALESCE(grouped_labs.total_tests, 0) AS total_labs 
FROM week_range 
LEFT OUTER JOIN grouped_labs ON week_range.week = grouped_labs.week
ORDER BY 1;

WITH top_counties AS (
    SELECT 
        s.cname AS county,
        AVG(s.avgscrread + s.avgscrmath + s.avgscrwrite) AS avg_total_sat
    FROM 
        satscores s
    WHERE 
        s.avgscrread IS NOT NULL 
        AND s.avgscrmath IS NOT NULL 
        AND s.avgscrwrite IS NOT NULL
        AND s.rtype = 'S'  -- Only include school-level records
    GROUP BY 
        s.cname
    ORDER BY 
        avg_total_sat DESC
    LIMIT 5
)
SELECT 
    s.cname AS county,
    s.sname AS school_name,
    s.dname AS district_name,
    s.avgscrread AS avg_reading,
    s.avgscrmath AS avg_math,
    s.avgscrwrite AS avg_writing,
    (s.avgscrread + s.avgscrmath + s.avgscrwrite) AS total_sat
FROM 
    satscores s
JOIN 
    top_counties tc ON s.cname = tc.county
WHERE 
    s.avgscrread IS NOT NULL 
    AND s.avgscrmath IS NOT NULL 
    AND s.avgscrwrite IS NOT NULL
    AND s.rtype = 'S'  -- Only include school-level records
ORDER BY 
    s.cname, 
    total_sat DESC;

SELECT 
    c.curriculum_id,
    COUNT(DISTINCT unnested_values) AS distinct_count
FROM (
    SELECT 
        c.curriculum_id,
        unnest(ARRAY[
            e."Content_Type"::text, 
            e."Platform_Used"::text, 
            cr.recommended::text
        ]) AS unnested_values
    FROM curriculum_resources cr
    JOIN educational_resources e ON cr.resource_key = e.resource_key
    JOIN curricula c ON c.curriculum_id = cr.curriculum_id
) AS subquery
GROUP BY c.curriculum_id
ORDER BY c.curriculum_id;

-- First, ensure the status column exists
ALTER TABLE curricula 
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS status TEXT DEFAULT 'installed';

-- Recursive CTE to find all dependent curricula
WITH RECURSIVE curricula_to_upgrade AS (
    -- Base case: start with the specific curriculum
    SELECT curriculum_id
    FROM curricula
    WHERE curriculum_id = 1  -- "Introduction to Programming" ID
      AND status = 'installed'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: find curricula that depend on resources from already identified curricula
    SELECT cr.curriculum_id
    FROM curriculum_resources cr
    JOIN curricula c ON cr.curriculum_id = c.curriculum_id
    JOIN curricula_to_upgrade ctu ON cr.resource_key IN (
        -- Find resources associated with curricula we've already identified
        SELECT cr2.resource_key
        FROM curriculum_resources cr2
        WHERE cr2.curriculum_id = ctu.curriculum_id
    )
    WHERE c.status = 'installed'  -- Only include curricula that haven't been upgraded yet
)
-- Update the status of all identified curricula
UPDATE curricula
SET status = 'to upgrade'
WHERE curriculum_id IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT curriculum_id 
    FROM curricula_to_upgrade
);

WITH time_series AS (
  SELECT 
    generate_series(
      date_trunc('hour', min(start_timestamp)),
      date_trunc('hour', max(end_timestamp)),
      '1 hour'::interval
    ) AS hour
  FROM event_usage
  WHERE start_timestamp BETWEEN '2024-10-01' AND '2024-10-09'
),
valid_hours AS (
  SELECT hour
  FROM time_series
  WHERE 
    EXTRACT(HOUR FROM hour) BETWEEN 9 AND 17 AND
    EXTRACT(DOW FROM hour) BETWEEN 1 AND 5
),
usage_hours AS (
  SELECT 
    e.learner_code,
    e.resource_key,
    v.hour,
    LEAST(
      EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM LEAST(e.end_timestamp, (v.hour + interval '1 hour'))),
      EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (v.hour + interval '1 hour'))
    ) - 
    GREATEST(
      EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM GREATEST(e.start_timestamp, v.hour)),
      EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM v.hour)
    ) AS seconds_in_hour
  FROM event_usage e
  JOIN valid_hours v ON 
    v.hour >= date_trunc('hour', e.start_timestamp) AND 
    v.hour < date_trunc('hour', e.end_timestamp)
  WHERE e.start_timestamp BETWEEN '2024-10-01' AND '2024-10-09'
)
SELECT 
  learner_code, 
  resource_key, 
  SUM(seconds_in_hour) AS total_working_hours_time_spent
FROM usage_hours
WHERE seconds_in_hour > 0
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key
ORDER BY learner_code, resource_key;

WITH filled_ratings AS (
  SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    usage_date,
    rating,
    LAST_VALUE(rating) IGNORE NULLS OVER (
      PARTITION BY learner_code, resource_key 
      ORDER BY usage_date 
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS filled_rating
  FROM 
    resource_usage_2
)
SELECT 
  learner_code,
  resource_key,
  usage_date,
  rating AS original_rating,
  filled_rating
FROM 
  filled_ratings
ORDER BY 
  learner_code, 
  resource_key, 
  usage_date;

WITH ranked_ratings AS (
  SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    usage_date,
    rating,
    MAX(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN usage_date END) OVER (
      PARTITION BY learner_code, resource_key
      ORDER BY usage_date
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS last_rating_date
  FROM 
    resource_usage_2
)
SELECT 
  r.learner_code,
  r.resource_key,
  r.usage_date,
  r.rating AS original_rating,
  COALESCE(r.rating, prev.rating) AS filled_rating
FROM 
  ranked_ratings r
LEFT JOIN 
  resource_usage_2 prev 
  ON r.learner_code = prev.learner_code
  AND r.resource_key = prev.resource_key
  AND r.last_rating_date = prev.usage_date
ORDER BY 
  r.learner_code, 
  r.resource_key, 
  r.usage_date;

WITH date_series AS (
  SELECT dd::date AS day_date
  FROM generate_series('2022-01-01'::timestamp, '2022-01-31'::timestamp, '1 day'::interval) dd
)
SELECT 
  ds.day_date,
  COALESCE(SUM(
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (
      LEAST(incident_end, (ds.day_date + interval '1 day')::timestamp) - 
      GREATEST(incident_start, ds.day_date::timestamp)
    ))/60
  ), 0) AS incident_minutes
FROM date_series ds
LEFT JOIN race_incidents ri ON 
  ds.day_date <= ri.incident_end::date AND 
  (ds.day_date + interval '1 day')::date > ri.incident_start::date
GROUP BY ds.day_date
ORDER BY ds.day_date;

SELECT p.id, p.sex, p.birthday, p.diagnosis,
       lab_data.ldh_1, lab_data.alp_1,
       lab_data.ldh_2, lab_data.alp_2,
       lab_data.ldh_3, lab_data.alp_3
FROM patient p
LEFT JOIN CROSSTAB(
    'SELECT l.id, 
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY l.id ORDER BY l.date) AS rn,
            l.ldh, l.alp
     FROM laboratory l
     ORDER BY 1, 2'
) AS lab_data (
    id BIGINT,
    ldh_1 BIGINT, alp_1 BIGINT,
    ldh_2 BIGINT, alp_2 BIGINT,
    ldh_3 BIGINT, alp_3 BIGINT
) ON p.id = lab_data.id
ORDER BY p.id;

WITH RankedResults AS (
    SELECT 
        r.driverid,
        r.raceid,
        r.points,
        r.time,
        r.rank,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY r.driverid ORDER BY r.points DESC) AS row_num
    FROM 
        results r
)
SELECT 
    rr.driverid,
    rr.raceid,
    rr.time,
    rr.rank,
    rr.points
FROM 
    RankedResults rr
ORDER BY 
    rr.driverid,
    rr.points ASC;

WITH RankedResults AS (
  SELECT 
    r.*,
    d.*,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY r.driverid ORDER BY r.points DESC) as driver_result_rank
  FROM 
    results r
  INNER JOIN 
    drivers d ON r.driverid = d.driverid
  WHERE 
    r.grid = 21
)
SELECT 
  * 
FROM 
  RankedResults
WHERE 
  driver_result_rank <= 3  -- Limit to top 3 results per driver
ORDER BY 
  points DESC;
